original_hypothesis.title: AutomataDcapV3Attestation Has No Ownership Transfer Mechanism
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: AutomataDcapV3Attestation uses a custom owner pattern set in the constructor with no transfer or renounce mechanism. Unlike EssentialContract-based contracts which inherit Ownable2Step, this contract's owner is permanently fixed. If the owner key is lost or compromised, all admin functions (TCB configuration, certificate revocation, QE identity, trusted enclave management) become permanently inaccessible or permanently controlled by the attacker.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: The deployment script (DeployOnL1.s.sol) deploys AutomataDcapV3Attestation without a proxy (noted: 'No need to proxy these'). The owner is set to the deployer in the constructor. There is no transferOwnership function. If Intel updates TCB info, revokes certificates, or the security landscape changes, the owner must update the contract. Permanent loss of this capability is operationally catastrophic.
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: DeployOnL1.s.sol deploys AutomataDcapV3Attestation directly (not proxied); owner is msg.sender; no ownership transfer in deployment script

proof_of_signal.pattern: owner = msg.sender in constructor; no transferOwnership, no renounceOwnership functions exist

preconditions: Owner private key is lost or compromised
Or owner needs to be transferred to a multisig/timelock for security

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: owner is write-once in constructor, no setter exists
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Compromised key gives permanent irrevocable control; lost key permanently freezes configuration
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Compromised owner can whitelist malicious enclaves, accept revoked certs, enable fraudulent attestations indefinitely

economic_questions: Is the current owner a multisig or EOA?
Can the contract be replaced via AddressManager registration?
What is the value at risk from permanent malicious control of attestation config?
validation_requirements: Check if rollupAddressManager can swap 'automata_dcap_attestation' address
Assess if swapping the registered address is sufficient mitigation
Evaluate operational risk of permanent owner lock

preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_profit_margin: High if SGX proving is economically significant

market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Undermines entire SGX proof verification tier

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: All protocol participants relying on SGX proofs

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: Current owner address and key management
investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Verify no hidden ownership transfer mechanism

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
